fields.panel1_dialog: The Magic Pointer turns 'This' and 'That' into commands — no typing!
fields.panel2_dialog: So it's not constantly tracking everywhere my cursor hovers... right?

narrative_summary: Google's own evangelist framing — 'just point at stuff!' — gets deflated by the immediate, obvious privacy horror that sparked the 'Don't hijack my mouse pointer' backlash the same day.
rationale: Candidate 3 earns the specificity point by quoting Google's actual branded name 'Magic Pointer' and the real product framing 'This' and 'That' (capitalized as in the blog), while also nodding to the documented 'Don't hijack my mouse pointer' backlash — grounding the joke in real-world reaction rather than generic surveillance anxiety. The template fit is ideal and the deadpan silence of panel 3 does the heavy lifting the others all share, but the extra specificity detail gives it the edge.
story_title: Reimagining the mouse pointer for the AI era
place: in a burning server room during a performance review
